subcommands.name: ssh
subcommands.description: Run shell or execute a command on a remote SSH node

options.description: Forces ssh to try protocol version 1 only
Forces ssh to try protocol version 2 only
Forces ssh to use IPv4 addresses only
Forces ssh to use IPv6 addresses only
Enables forwarding of the authentication agent connection
Disables forwarding of the authentication agent connection
Use bind_address on the local machine as the source address of the connection
Requests compression of all data (including stdin, stdout, stderr, and data for forwarded X11 and TCP connections)
Selects the cipher specification for encrypting the session
Specifies a local 'dynamic' application-level port forwarding
Sets the escape character for sessions with a pty (default: '~')
Specifies an alternative per-user configuration file
Requests ssh to go to background just before command execution
Allows remote hosts to connect to local forwarded ports
Specify the PKCS#11 shared library ssh should use to communicate with a PKCS#11 token providing the user's private RSA key
Selects a file from which the identity (private key) for public key authentication is read
Enables GSSAPI-based authentication and forwarding (delegation) of GSSAPI credentials to the server
Disables forwarding (delegation) of GSSAPI credentials to the server
Specifies that the given port on the local (client) host is to be forwarded to the given host and port on the remote side

arg.name: port:host:hostport
arg.description: Local port, followed by host and host port to forward to

options.description: Specifies the user to log in as on the remote machine

arg.name: login name
arg.description: Name of user logging into remote machine

options.description: Places the ssh client into ``master'' mode for connection sharing
Additionally, for protocol version 2 a comma-separated list of MAC (message authentication code) algorithms can be specified in order of preference
Do not execute a remote command
Redirects stdin from /dev/null (actually, prevents reading from stdin)
Control an active connection multiplexing master process

arg.name: ctl cmd
arg.description: Command that's passed to the master process

options.description: Can be used to give options in the format used in the configuration file

arg.name: option
arg.description: Options that can be specified in the format of the config file

options.description: Port to connect to on the remote host
Quiet mode.  Causes most warning and diagnostic messages to be suppressed
Specifies that the given port on the remote (server) host is to be forwarded to the given host and port on the local side

arg.name: port:host:hostport
arg.description: Local port, followed by host and host port to forward to

options.description: Specifies the location of a control socket for connection sharing, or the string 'none' to disable connection sharing
May be used to request invocation of a subsystem on the remote system
Display the version number and exit
Verbose mode.  Causes ssh to print debugging messages about its progress
Requests that standard input and output on the client be forwarded to host on port over the secure channel

arg.name: host:port
arg.description: Host and port to forward to

options.description: Requests tunnel device forwarding with the specified tun(4) devices between the client (local_tun) and the server (remote_tun)
Send log information using the syslog(3) system module

args.name: user@hostname
args.description: Address of remote machine to log into

options.description: Minutes to live for a SSH session
Do not verify server's certificate and host name. Use only in test environments
Specify the name of authentication connector to use
Skip version checking between server and client
Controls how keys are handled. Valid values are [auto no yes only]
Enable support for SSH escape sequences. Type '~?' during an SSH session to list supported sequences. Default is enabled
Override host:port used when opening a browser for cluster logins
Minutes to live for a SSH session
Do not verify server's certificate and host name. Use only in test environments
Specify the name of authentication connector to use
Skip version checking between server and client
Controls how keys are handled. Valid values are [auto no yes only]
Enable support for SSH escape sequences. Type '~?' during an SSH session to list supported sequences. Default is enabled
Override host:port used when opening a browser for cluster logins
